conversations.content: For repositories owned by me, remove my stars if they have also been starred by other users.

<function_thought>
To achieve the task of removing stars from repositories owned by the user that have also been starred by other users, I will follow the initialized function trajectory. First, I will retrieve the logged-in GitHub user's information, which includes details like their repositories. Next, I will gather information on those repositories to check which ones have been starred by other users and then proceed to remove the stars from those repositories.
</function_thought>

<function_call>[get_logged_in_github_user_information()]</function_call>

<final_answer></final_answer>

<function_thought>
The logged-in user, Emma Johnson, has the following repositories: 
1. `file_compression`
2. `image_transformation`
3. `linear_algebra_operation`

She has starred the repository `file_compression`. However, I need to check if this repository or any other owned by her has also been starred by other users before removing the stars. According to the information, she has only starred her own repository, which means there will be no stars to remove since it hasn't been starred by others. 

I will still retrieve the detailed information about her repositories in order to verify the starred status by other users.
</function_thought>

<function_call>[get_github_repository_information(repo_name='emmajohnson/file_compression'), get_github_repository_information(repo_name='emmajohnson/image_transformation'), get_github_repository_information(repo_name='emmajohnson/linear_algebra_operation')]</function_call>
